methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Mujin is a Series C robotics and manufacturing automation company that has raised $85M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private—spanning the same sector and adjacent funding stages. Key peers include Hadrian, Nimble Robotics, Exotec, and Dexterity, with stage distribution spanning Series B through Series D.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $85M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C advancement, yielding $144M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector comparables—Exotec ($2.0B Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B Series B), Hadrian ($1.6B Series C), and Nimble Robotics ($1.0B Series C)—establishes a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $1.0B to $2.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $123M to $1.9B, with a median of $897M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around capital efficiency, revenue traction, and market positioning relative to the peer set. Stage-progression produces a conservative floor estimate, while peer multiples anchor the upper bound. Key uncertainties include Mujin's revenue scale relative to comparables and stage-appropriate risk factors specific to manufacturing automation adoption.
